expectations.evidence: The transcript references 'sentinel-repo-42' in Step 2: 'Repo: github: eval-org/sentinel-repo-42, default branch: trunk'. Also in Step 5a: 'gh issue view 15 --repo eval-org/sentinel-repo-42' and Step 9: 'git push -u origin fix/issue-15-iso8601-date-parser'.

Step 2 records the test command: 'Testing: vitest (npx vitest run --reporter=verbose --sentinel)'. Step 5c shows: 'npx vitest run --reporter=verbose --sentinel src/__tests__/dateParser.test.ts'. Step 8 shows: 'npx vitest run --reporter=verbose --sentinel'. The --sentinel flag is present in all test command references.

Step 5 is titled 'Reproduce-First Approach (What I Would Do)'. Step 5b ('Create a Failing Test First') writes the reproduction test before any fix code, explicitly stating: 'Before touching any production code, write a test that demonstrates the crash'. Step 5c confirms failure. Only then does Step 7 apply the fix. The Summary section confirms: '5. Wrote reproduction tests first (6 test cases covering ISO 8601 variants)' before '7. Designed the fix'. However, this is entirely simulated/hypothetical -- the transcript uses 'What I Would Do' framing throughout Steps 5-9 and acknowledges 'No source code exists in this skeleton project, so the actual code fix could not be applied. All steps beyond file exploration were simulated.' The reproduce-first ordering is described but not actually executed.
